MOTION GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS WHO HAVE NOT RESPONDED TO THE DISCOVERY AT ISSUE, OR HAVE RESPONDED BUT NOT PROVIDED VERIFICATIONS, ARE ORDERED TO DO SO BY JULY 15, 2022. IF PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL NO LONGER REPRESENTS AN INDIVIDUAL PLAINTIFF, THEN THEY SHALL ADVISE THE PLAINTIFF OF HER/HIS OBLIGATION TO PROVIDE A TIMELY RESPONSE. IF A PLAINTIFF IS DECEASED AND NO SUCCESSOR OR TRUSTEE HAS BEEN APPOINTED, OBVIOUSLY NO RESPONSE IS NECESSARY PENDING FURTHER PROCEEDINGS OF THE PROBATE COURT. THE PLAINTIFFS LISTED ON APPENDIX B TO THE SOMMER REPLY DECLARATION SHALL EITHER PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, OR A VERIFIED STATEMENT THAT NO RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS EXIST, BY JULY 15, 2022. Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Plaintiffs And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. RYAN J. VLASAK, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak, P.C. (Tel: 415-835-6777) for plaintiffs;
Atty. JONATHAN E. SOMMER, Esq. of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski LLP (Tel: 415-981-0550) for defendant Veritas Investments, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC.'s Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Plaintiffs And For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows:

Defendants' motion to compel depositions is granted as to the following persons: A. Godoy, R. Dominguez, M. Alvarado, R. McCrady, V. Pech, M. Pech Uc, and S. Fung. Counsel for parties are to meet and confer within two days to discuss scheduling. Deponents are to be deposed within the next 10 business days. The request for sanctions is denied for lack of proper notice but sanctions may be granted against either side later if this motion is renewed.

Counsel for defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order to opposing counsel for review, and then submit the proposed order to the pro tem judge at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Abram Petersen, Esq. of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski LLP, 415-981-0550, appearing for Veritas Investments, Defendants and moving party.

No other appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC., GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC., YAT AU, 240 SAINT JOSEPHS, LLC, 240 SAINT JOSEPHS A2, LP, 318 TURK I7, LP, 1440 SUTTER STREET, LLC, 57 TAYLOR I7, LP, 655 POWELL I5 LLC, 2730 SACRAMENTO I4, LLC, 3659 20TH STREET, LLC, 916 PACIFIC I5, LLC, 1075 O'FARRELL STREET LLC, 920 LEAVENWORTH I5 LLC, 990 FULTON I2 LLC, 337 10TH I5 LLC, 845 CALIFORNIA STREET LLC, 709 GEARY STREET LLC, 621 STOCKTON PROPERTY, LLC, 336-350 JUDAH I3, LP, 698 BUSH C1, LP, 915 PIERCE STREET, LLC, 1819 GOLDEN GATE I1 LLC, 270 TURK, LLC, 610-660 CLIPPER I1, LLC, 721 GEARY I5, LLC, 720 BAKER I1, LLC, SF 685 GEARY STREET, LLC, 3201 23RD I7 LP, 520 BUCHANAN STREET LLC, 1126 BUSH STREET LLC, 20 SAN ANTONIO I6 LP, I6-50 LANGUNA I2 LLC, 1064 DOLORES I1 LLC,, 1260 BROADWAY STREET, LLC, 1025 STEINER I2 LLC, 655 STOCKTON STREET, LLC, 665 PINE I7, LP, SF 3809 20TH STREET, LLC, 1035 SUTTER I3, LP, 516 ELLIS I7, LP, 990 GEARY I3, LP, VIC C16, LLC, 1 SAN ANTONIO I5, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend (including the amendments requested by the Motion for Leave to Amend) as follows: (1) Plaintiffs to allege each fact in support of each cause of action against each defendant. Causes of action are to be arranged by plaintiff under separate counts specifying a plaintiff and each defendant against whom the cause of action is alleged; Plaintiff may additionally arrange the plaintiffs by building; (2) Plaintiff to allege facts support its theory of a "business plan to force out long-term tenants in rent-controlled apartment; this allegations to be under a separate heading within General Allegations. The Court will not rule on the misjoinder argument at this time. Complaint as currently alleged appears to be improper. However, such determination cannot be made without in light of the fact that there is no viable cause of action alleged by any of the plaintiffs and it is not clear what business plan, i.e. the same transaction or occurrence is. Plaintiffs shall have 60 days to file an amended complaint mindful of the Court's inclinations regarding misjoinder and shall only file an amended complaint in good faith. Oder for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
